caveats: Suggestions and rewards are community-sourced and patch-volatile — verify in-game. Crew sizes are guidance, not hard limits.
Some activities are events (XenoThreat, Siege of Orison, Fleet Week) that only run during their window.
Unofficial — not affiliated with Cloud Imperium Games.

activities.name: Return of XenoThreat
activities.overview: XenoThreat is Star Citizen's flagship server-wide war event: the UEE Civilian Defence Force calls every able pilot to break a XenoThreat siege near INS Jericho. It's a multi-phase battle that swings from EVA salvage and a tense cargo-resupply convoy into a roaring fleet brawl against fighter waves, Hammerhead gunships and a capital-class Idris-M. It's tailor-made for a crew because the phases reward role-splitting: someone hauls crates, someone flies escort, someone mans turrets, and the whole server piles in around you for that big-battle spectacle.
roles: Combat pilot / interceptor — clear fighter waves and screen the convoy
Cargo runner — tractor crates from Starfarer wrecks to INS Jericho
Convoy escort / CAP — guard the hauler and the docked Javelin
Turret gunner & engineer on a multicrew gunship (Redeemer/Corsair)
Medic / logistics — C8R or Cutlass Red to revive and rearm downed pilots
ships: Gladius or F7C Hornet Mk II (agile fighters)
Ares Ion / Inferno (anti-capital firepower)
Aegis Redeemer or Drake Corsair (multicrew gun platform)
C2 Hercules / Cutlass for cargo resupply
C8R Pisces or Cutlass Red for medical support
Multi-tool with tractor-beam attachment for crate moving
activities.reward: Community guides cite up to ~450k aUEC across full participation, plus account-bound CDF flair tied to reputation: Veteran/Legendary CDF rank unlocks an exclusive CDF armor set (helmet, flight suit, chest), an RSI Spectrum badge, and military-grade component blueprints for repeat completions. aUEC figure is community-reported; flair/reputation rewards are officially confirmed.
activities.start: Watch for the XenoThreat priority/contract notification on your mobiGlas (F1) when the event is live and accept it to register with the CDF (this is what tracks your contribution points). Set your medical/respawn at the MIC-L1 R&R station or Port Tressler, then quantum to INS Jericho station in the MIC-L1 gas cloud and join the action. In-app, check the Field Guide / Tactics tabs for current phase and rep tips.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki (Fandom) — XenoThreat Returns / CDF ALERT: Attack on XenoThreat
RSI Spectrum — XenoThreat Returns: CDF Reputation Rewards (official announcement)
MMOPIXEL — Star Citizen 4.8 Return of XenoThreat Guide (phases, location, rewards)
Join the Verse — XenoThreat Guide (cargo types, start, combat roles)
RSI Community Hub — XenoThreat Guide: Tips, Rewards (up to 450k aUEC)

activities.name: Tactical Strike Group (InterSec, Nyx)
activities.overview: SC's first purpose-built co-op combat operation, dropped in Alpha 4.8 by InterSec Defense Solutions in the Nyx system. One scripted, five-phase mission chain that throws everything at a crew in sequence: hold off fighter waves to defend the friendly Idris "Tranquility," crack four armored power relays with ballistics only, fly a tight asteroid trench run to blow the cooling units and core, then dismount and clear the station on foot to rescue a captured pilot, capped by a ship-to-ship brawl against a capital-class Vanduul Mauler. It is the rare SC activity that genuinely demands distinct roles and live comms, so a tight small crew that splits jobs cleanly will feel like a real strike team.
roles: Flight lead / interceptor (clears fighter waves, screens the capital)
Ballistic gunship (brings dedicated ballistic guns for the armored relays)
Trench-run pilot (agile fighter for the tunnel + cooling units)
Dropship + FPS boarder (lands the ground team, clears the station, escorts the pilot)
Capital / sub-cap anchor (sustained DPS and tanking vs the Vanduul Mauler)
ships: Capital/sub-cap anchor: Polaris, Perseus, Paladin, or an Idris
Ballistic + heavy: Corsair, Constellation Andromeda, Vanguard, Redeemer
Light/medium fighters: Gladius, Arrow, Buccaneer, F7 Hornet, F8 Lightning
Dropship for the ground team: Cutlass, Valkyrie, or similar with a seated FPS squad
activities.reward: aUEC payout plus chance-based blueprint drops (one random blueprint per run from two pools): racing flight suits/helmets, Novia Crossbow variants, Omnisky weapons and power plants, high-grade ship components, and a unique bomber jacket. No official aUEC figure published — treat amounts as unconfirmed.
activities.start: Build standing with InterSec Defense Solutions in Nyx (you need at least Contractor / Rank III). Then open mobiGlass (F1) → Contracts → Mercenary tab and accept "Tactical Strike Group Needed" under Verified Contracts. Three intro contracts all funnel to the same QV Extraction Station in Nyx. In-app, the Tactics tab covers the phase-by-phase playbook and role assignments.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Tactical Strike Group
RSI Comm-Link — Alpha 4.8: Tactical Strike
Citizen Starter Guide — Tactical Strike Groups Guide (phases, fleet, rewards)
ExpCarry — Star Citizen 4.8 Tactical Strike Guide

activities.name: Pyro Contested Zone Raid (Ruin / Checkmate / Orbituary)
activities.type: contested-zone raid / FPS PvPvE loot run
activities.overview: The Contested Zones are free-fire PvPvE deathtraps buried in three Pyro asteroid stations — Checkmate, Orbituary and Ruin (the Ghost Arena) — where you fight NPCs and rival crews for rare loot. The real prize is the 7 Com Boards: collect them across the stations, jam them into the Executive Hangar pipes, and you crack open a hangar holding a reward ship pre-fitted with Grade-A components, plus rare FPS guns. It's a blast for a small crew because the keycard/fuse-door maze, NPC waves and other players hunting you mean someone always has to watch the back door while someone else cracks the lock.
roles: Breach lead (point man, clears NPC rooms, opens fuse/blue doors)
Cover / rear guard (watches flanks and the door you came in, revives downed crew)
Keycard runner (grabs Red/Blue cards from printers and supervisor terminals, fetches Com Boards)
Loadmaster / mule (hauls boards, loot, ammo back to the ship and feeds the hangar pipes)
Pilot / escort (keeps the ship parked safe, fends off interceptors outside, evacs the haul)
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (cheap drop-ship, big door, medic bed)
Anvil Carrack or C2 Hercules (group transport + beds + cargo for the haul)
MISC Fortune / lighter ship as a quick keycard ferry
A fighter (Gladius / F7A) flying escort to keep the parked ship alive
activities.reward: High but variable — and death means losing what you carry. Executive Hangar contested-zone raids drop high-tier loot: Grade-A ship components, rare FPS weapons (grenade launchers, railguns), plus lootable RMC/CMAS, illicit goods and components — potentially worth a lot if you extract and sell it. Payout is “what you walk out with” (community-reported; no fixed reward).
activities.start: Fly to Pyro and dock at a contested-zone station (Checkmate, Orbituary or Ruin). Some areas need a Red keycard pulled from a Supervisor outpost/terminal first; inside you cycle airlocks, clear NPC rooms and use fuse doors (30s window) plus printed Blue cards to push deeper. Work toward the 7 Com Boards, then turn them in at an Executive Hangar — note the hangar only stays open ~12 minutes once unlocked, so the whole crew must be ready to grab-and-go. No mission-giver; it's open-world, just travel there.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Contested Zones
Star Citizen Wiki - Guide: Executive Hangars
Star Citizen Wiki - Checkmate Station
Survivor To Hero - Pyro Contested Zones Quick Start Guide
Survivor To Hero - Executive Hangar Guide
swtorstrategies - Contested Zones Guide: Tips, Rewards, Survival

activities.name: Onyx Facility Co-op Clear
activities.type: UGF dungeon clear / FPS loot run
activities.overview: Drop a fireteam into one of the ~100 abandoned ASD "Onyx" underground research facilities scattered across Stanton's moons, ride the elevator down past the armistice lobby, and fight room-by-room through raider NPCs and a balcony Quick-Response Force while clearing loot boxes for weapons, armor and components. It's tense, claustrophobic dungeon-style FPS that genuinely rewards splitting up to cover angles, revive teammates and haul caches — friends report it's some of the most coherent co-op fun in the game. Mind the decay hazards: radiation pits and structural traps kill the careless faster than the NPCs do.
roles: Point/breacher — leads room entries, draws fire, prioritizes the QRF that spawns on the atrium balcony
Overwatch/sniper — holds long sightlines and covers the breacher with a Volt Zenith or similar
Medic/recovery — carries med-gel and a med tool, revives downed crew, manages radiation exposure
Loot/mule — works boxes and caches, mines through any blocking stalactites/debris, hauls drives and containers to the ship
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (cheap multicrew dropship + cargo for caches)
Anvil Valkyrie or C8R Pisces (troop drop / on-site medical)
Aegis Avenger Titan (budget solo seat to the rally point)
MISC Prospector or a handheld mining tool only if a path is blocked by minable rock
activities.reward: Variable, tag as community: NPC and box loot (e.g. Parallax rifles, Volt Zenith sniper, Geist armor sets, weapon attachments, ship components at some sites). Storyline phases pay in ASD Secure Drives (5 in Phase 1, 10 in Phase 2) and ASD Compounds, redeemable at Wikelo's Emporium rather than flat aUEC. Real value is in resold gear/armor — verify current prices in-app, payouts shift between patches.
activities.start: Open mobiGlas, go to the Contracts/Comms-Link missions and pick up the Onyx investigation chain from Arken Mallor (Hockrow Agency, Investigation tab); mercenary/bounty variants also exist via Eckhart, the Bounty Hunters Guild and Redwind. Accepting spawns a quantum marker to a facility on a Stanton moon (Cellin, Lyria, Calliope, etc.) — there's no other direct access. Note: in current builds only the mission-holder banks the contract reward, so each crew member who wants payout should take their own contract; loot in hand is shared freely.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Onyx Facility (ASD)
RSI Community Hub — Onyx Facility Looting Guide
Solar Trade Alliance — Onyx Facilities FAQ
MMOPixel — ASD Onyx Facilities Guide (Phases, Contracts, Loot)
MMOPixel — SC 4.8 Best Loot Farming Guide
Scopique — Losing It in the Onyx Facilities (player co-op account)

activities.name: Security Bunker Assault
activities.overview: A planetside breach-and-clear against a turret-defended underground bunker packed with 6-8 hostile gangsters. It's the friendliest on-ramp into Star Citizen FPS co-op: short, repeatable, and tense in the best way. The twist on the "defend" variant is that security NPCs are fighting alongside you, so the real challenge is target ID and trigger discipline, not raw aim, which makes it perfect for teaching a new crewmate the ropes.
roles: Pilot/door-watch: lands behind cover to dodge rooftop turrets, then guards the entrance and the ship
Point/breacher: leads stack into rooms, calls targets
Support/overwatch: covers angles, revives, watches for friendly NPCs to avoid mission-failing friendly fire
ships: Anvil Pisces or RAFT/Cutlass for cheap 2-4 person drop
Drake Cutlass Black (drops a trio plus loot)
Aegis Avenger Titan or any solo fighter for a duo
Ground vehicle (Cyclone/ROC-buggy) optional for moon hops between bunkers
activities.reward: Roughly 15,000-22,500 aUEC per contract (community-reported for the 6-10 hostile variants); scales with difficulty/number of buildings. Plus lootable weapons, armor and ammo from downed NPCs.
activities.start: Open mobiGlas > Contract Manager and accept a Mercenary/Security bunker contract (givers like Crusader Security, Hurston Dynamics/Wallace Klim, or "Defend Occupants" from local security). Party up first and have the contract holder share it so the whole crew gets credit. Bunkers sit on Crusader moons (Daymar NT-999-XVI, Cellin NT-999-XV, Yela NT-999-XXII), across Hurston (~15 sites), microTech, and ArcCorp's moons Lyria/Wala.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Defend Occupants (friendly-NPC clear mission, party-wide fail on friendly fire)
RSI Community Hub - How To Ace Bunker Missions (turret evasion, Hurston/microTech bunkers)
Star Citizen Wiki - Contract/Mission (mobiGlas Contract Manager, bunker mercenary missions ~10 enemies)
Star Citizen Wiki - NT-999-XVI Daymar bunker (Nine Tails, rooftop turret, restricted area)
Gamerant - Star Citizen Mercenary Guide (bunker clear, ~10 hostiles, more can spawn, reward variants)

activities.name: Distribution Center Loot Run
activities.overview: Covalex Distribution Centres are sprawling multi-floor indoor complexes scattered across the planets and moons of Stanton, defended by armed hostile NPCs across tiered Security 1 and Security 2 zones. In 4.8 CIG quietly buffed the loot tables so the hidden orange boxes now drop genuinely useful ship components alongside weapons and armor, turning them into a great FPS payday for a small crew. It's fun for a team because one fortified site is too much to sweep alone — splitting up to clear floors, cover lifts, and dig out vent- and maintenance-route loot rooms while someone watches the door is exactly the kind of coordinated room-clearing that solo players can't match.
roles: Breach lead (point man, clears rooms first)
Flank/cover (watches lifts, stairwells and angles)
Looter (sweeps orange boxes, manages backpack space)
Door watch / overwatch (guards exits and spots incoming players)
Pilot/getaway (keeps the ship hot for extraction)
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (cheap dropship, hauls a full squad + loot)
Anvil Valkyrie or C8R/medical ship for in-field rezzing
RSI Constellation Taurus or other mid cargo hauler for loot capacity
A nimble fighter (e.g. M80, Gladius) for escort if PvP-contested
activities.reward: Community-reported as "good value" rather than fixed cash: looted weapons, full armor sets, and (new in 4.8) ship components such as coolers, power plants, shields and quantum drives that can be equipped or sold/used for crafting. Treat per-run aUEC value as variable and unconfirmed — the draw is free gear/components, not a guaranteed payout.
activities.start: No mission contract needed — fly directly to any Covalex Distribution Centre (e.g. S1DC06 on Hurston, S4DC05 on microTech) and infiltrate on foot; some also offer cargo/raid/incursion contracts via the mission manager. Land nearby, breach the corporate atrium (reception/security desks + lifts), then push through the Security 1 and Security 2 areas into the central facility, looting hidden orange boxes reachable via maintenance routes, vents, drops and jumping paths. In-app, see the Field Guide for the location and Gear for what you're hunting.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Distribution centers (layout, security tiers, orange loot boxes)
Star Citizen Wiki — Covalex Distribution Centre S1DC06 (Hurston)
Star Citizen Wiki — Covalex Distribution Centre S4DC05 (microTech)
MMOPIXEL — Star Citizen 4.8 Best Loot Farming Guide (4.8 component buff, FPS looting tips)
YouTube — 4.8 Quietly Updated Distribution Centers with Loot! (Star Citizen)
YouTube — Distribution Center Infiltration Guide and Full Palatino Run

activities.name: Group Bounty Hunting (Shared ERT Contracts)
activities.overview: One crew member pulls a high-tier bounty contract and hits "Share" in mobiGlas, so the whole party gets the same target plus an even split of the payout and Bounty Hunters Guild reputation. ERT (Extreme Risk Target) contracts throw sub-capital ships and packs of heavy escorts at you that you genuinely should not solo, so it turns into a satisfying squad dogfight where light fighters tag and chase while heavier ships and turrets finish the kills. It is the cleanest way for a small group to all rank up the same Guild reputation track together in one sitting.
roles: Wing lead / interdiction - flies a nimble fighter, calls targets and keeps escorts pinned in arc
Heavy DPS / finisher - flies the gunship or torpedo boat to land the killing blows
Turret gunner(s) - mans manned turrets on the multi-crew ship to punish evasive escorts
Engineer / repair-loot - manages power, repairs in-flight, grabs any salvage or drops
ships: Aegis Hammerhead (6 manned turrets, ideal anchor for repeated ERTs with a full crew)
RSI Constellation (turret + crew, combines bounty hunting with looting)
Drake Corsair (heavy guns + cargo for loot runs)
Aegis Vanguard (durable anti-'I keep dying' pick)
Light/heavy fighters - Anvil Hornet, RSI Scorpius, Anvil Hurricane (Hurricane and Scorpius gain a lot from a friend in the gunner seat)
activities.reward: Rough community estimates: VHRT ~60k+ aUEC and ERT ~100k+ aUEC per contract before the split; a coordinated squad running back-to-back ERTs nets roughly 150k-250k aUEC per person per session depending on target tier and efficiency. Reputation is shared on top. Figures are community-reported and fluctuate by patch (Alpha 4.8.x).
activities.start: Each crew member first needs Bounty Hunters Guild access - complete the Guild certification mission(s) (entry fee scales with tier) to unlock contracts in the Crusader or Hurston jurisdiction. Accept a contract from a Guild/Contract terminal or the mobiGlas Contracts app, open the contract detail and press the "Share" button while partied so it copies to everyone with the reward and rep split evenly. Higher tiers (VHRT/ERT) unlock by clearing lower tiers and periodic verification missions. In-app this lives under the Earnings tab (with combat tactics in the Tactics tab).

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Contract (mobiGlas Share button, even reward/rep split)
Star Citizen Wiki - Bounty Hunters Guild (certification, tiers, jurisdictions)
Star Citizen Wiki Fandom - Bounty Issued For (ERT) - extreme-risk sub-capital targets
Game Truth - SC Beginner Money Guide 2026 (VHRT/ERT payout ranges)
ExpCarry - SC Best Ships for Missions (Patch 4.8)
MMOPIXEL - SC Best Bounty Hunting Ships & Strategies (multi-crew, Hammerhead, group tactics)
Game Rant - Star Citizen Bounty Hunter Guide (ERT requires group, escorts)

activities.name: Multi-Crew Dogfight Wing
activities.overview: Pile your whole crew into a single multi-crew hull and turn it into a flying weapons platform: one player flies and works the nose guns while the others man the turrets, missiles and power management. A Cutlass Black goes from a so-so solo fighter to a genuine brawler the moment a second person hops in the turret, and a Constellation or Hammerhead becomes a 360-degree wall of fire that no single-seat fighter wants to tangle with. It is the purest "teamwork-in-one-hull" loop in the game and a great way for a new crew to learn how to fight together.
roles: Pilot - flies the ship, manages throttle/boost, lines up nose guns and missile locks
Turret gunner(s) - tracks fast movers the pilot can't, holds rear/flank arcs
Co-pilot / engineer - power triage, shield/weapon balancing, calls out incoming and repairs
Missile officer / spare gunner - manages missile racks and jumps to a free turret or repairs hull
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (2 crew: pilot + 1 turret)
RSI Constellation Andromeda (works best with 3-4: pilot + dual S6 turrets + co-pilot)
Aegis Hammerhead (the big swing: 6 turrets, wants 4-5+ to shine as an anti-fighter gunbus)
activities.reward: For-fun first, but pairs naturally with combat bounties: a coordinated crew can chain HRT/VHRT/ERT contracts, and ERT-tier targets are reported around 100k+ aUEC each. Community estimates put a smooth squad near 150k-250k aUEC per person/hour. Numbers vary by patch and target tier (community).
activities.start: No special unlock to just fly together - spawn the multi-crew ship, assign seats, and pick a fight (combat dynamic events, a contested area, or a sparring run). To put money behind it, pull combat contracts from the Bounty Hunters Guild via the mobiGlas Contract Manager; higher tiers (HRT/VHRT/ERT) are the ones that actually reward a full crew. See the in-app Tactics tab for the role-split and the Earnings/Trade tab for bounty payouts.

sources.label: Cutlass Black - Star Citizen Wiki (manned turret, 2 crew)
Constellation Andromeda - Star Citizen Wiki (dual S6 turrets, crew)
Hammerhead - Star Citizen Wiki (six S4 turrets, gunship crew)
Bounty Hunters Guild - Star Citizen Wiki (ERT requires a crew, sub-cap targets)
Star Citizen Beginner Money Guide 2026 - Game Truth (group bounty earnings)

activities.name: Group Ship Mining on a MOLE (3 Laser Turrets)
activities.overview: The Argo MOLE is the game's purpose-built multicrew miner: three independent size-2 laser turrets that only sing when each one has a body in the seat. One player flies and scans for the juicy rocks while the gunners crack and extract in parallel, so there's almost no idle time between fractures — a real production line instead of one person doing everything. It's a relaxed, talky loop (call your power levels, warn before a rock pops) that rewards coordination and quietly stacks up serious credits over a session.
roles: Pilot/scanner — positions the ship, scans for high-value rocks, watches for threats
Crack gunner — runs the primary laser, manages the fracture power window
Stability/support gunner — feeds stability gadgets/modules to tame unstable rocks
Cleanup/extraction gunner (4th) — works leftover fragments so nothing is wasted
Optional 5th: escort/hauler pilot in a separate ship to guard pods and run cash-outs
ships: Argo MOLE (the core platform — 3 turrets, 96 SCU active across 8 pods)
Optional escort fighter for hot/contested areas
Optional cargo hauler to ferry jettisoned pods on long sessions
activities.reward: Roughly 500K–1M aUEC/hr is a common community ballpark when targeting refined Quantanium, scaling with crew coordination and luck on rock spawns; lower for safer/common ores. Note: there is no native in-game payout split, so crews divide manually (e.g. transport beacon workaround) or rotate who cashes out. (community)
activities.start: Spawn or rent an Argo MOLE (rental terminals at major spaceports; otherwise pledge/in-game purchase), crew up the three turrets plus pilot, then fly 5+ km above a moon/planet surface or into an asteroid belt like the Aaron Halo. Hit scan (Tab), pulse to find minable rocks, target high-value ones (Quantanium for max value/risk), fracture into the green energy zone without overcharging, then extract the purple fragments. In our app, the Optimizer tab helps dial in laser/module loadouts before you launch.

sources.label: MOLE — Star Citizen Wiki (ship design, 3 turrets, crew, pods)
Argo MOLE Guide: Crew Mining Workflow & Solo Reality — LTI Hangar
Star Citizen 4.8 Complete Mining Guide — MMOPixel
Star Citizen Mining Guide 2026: Find, Fracture, Refine, Sell — GGWTB (payout ballpark)
Guide:Mining — Star Citizen Wiki (scan/fracture/extract mechanics)

activities.name: Combined Ship + ROC Ground Mining Expedition
activities.overview: Set up a one-stop mining camp on a moon or planet surface: land a multicrew miner near a deposit field, then fan your crew out across every tier of mining at once. One team works the big ore veins from the ship's mining turrets while buddies drive a wheeled ROC to crack medium surface rocks and others hand-mine the scattered gem clusters on foot. It's a relaxed, talkative "everyone's busy doing their own thing in the same spot" loop that scales naturally from a duo to a full squad and turns a quiet moon into a shared payday.
roles: Miner pilot/spotter: land the MOLE on stable ground, scan deposits, call out rock locations and watch for threats
Ship turret operator(s): work the deep ore veins from the MOLE's mining lasers, manage the power gauge to fracture without overcharging
ROC driver: deploy the wheeled ROC to crack and extract the mid-size surface boulders
Hand miner(s): roam on foot with the Multi-Tool + OreBit attachment to harvest gem clusters (Hadanite, Aphorite, Dolivine)
Hauler/runner (optional 5th): ferry full ROC/backpack loads back to the ship and consolidate cargo for the sell run
ships: ARGO MOLE (multicrew miner: pilot + 3 turret operators)
Greycat ROC or ROC-DS (wheeled surface mining vehicle)
Pyro RYT Multi-Tool with OreBit Mining Attachment (hand mining)
Optional transport with a wide ramp to carry the ROC: Freelancer MAX, Cutlass Black, or Valkyrie
MISC Prospector as a lighter solo-miner alternative if no MOLE is available
activities.reward: Variable, scales with crew and time. Hand-mined gems: a full backpack of mostly Hadanite sells for roughly 50,000-60,000 aUEC and refills in ~30 min, so an efficient hand miner can clear 100,000+ aUEC/hr. ROC and MOLE ore stacks add substantially on top, especially after refining. Treat figures as community estimates that shift each patch.
activities.start: Pick a gem-rich body (Aberdeen is the most consistent Hadanite spot in 4.8; Lyria, Wala, Cellin, and Daymar are good for Aphorite). Load a ROC into a transport (or fly the MOLE direct), bring Multi-Tools with OreBit attachments, and bring proper armor for hot moons. Land near a dense cluster, set up the camp, then sell raw or refined ore at the nearest mining outpost or refinery station. In-app, see the Field Guide for mining mechanics and the Earnings/Trade tabs for current gem prices and sell points.

sources.label: SC Wiki - MOLE (multicrew miner, pilot + 3 operators, 3 turrets)
SC Wiki - OreBit Mining Attachment (hand mining via Multi-Tool)
SC Wiki - ROC (wheeled surface mining vehicle)
MMOPIXEL - ROC Mining Guide (prospect/fracture/extract, gem values)
MMOPIXEL - Hand Mining Beginners Guide (gems, backpack payouts)
LTI Hangar - ARGO MOLE crew mining workflow guide
hadanite.com - 4.8 Hadanite/Aphorite locations (Aberdeen, Lyria, Wala, etc.)
Star Citizen Help - Mining Career Guide (ship vs ROC vs FPS mining)

activities.name: Reclaimer Heavy Salvage Operation
activities.overview: Crew the Aegis Reclaimer, the size-6 heavy salvager whose big front "claw" can fracture and disintegrate whole wrecks into Recycled Material Composite (RMC). One person can technically fly it, but the ship was built for a team: with a pilot parking on target, two salvage stations chewing hulls, and a dedicated hand managing the filler stations and the cargo grid, you get the full salvage-company fantasy and far better throughput. Great chill, talky industry session where everyone has a clear job and the aUEC piles up.
roles: Pilot — find and park on derelicts/panels, hold position, watch radar
Salvage operator A — run the claw: hull-scrape, then Fracture/Disintegrate mode to munch chunks into RMC
Salvage operator B — second salvage beam/station for parallel scraping and faster munching
Cargo & filler boss — work the two filler stations, package 2/8/16 SCU crates and pack the 420-SCU grid
Escort/turret gunner (5th or 2nd ship) — man a side turret or fly a fighter to deter pirates and tractor stray crates
ships: Aegis Reclaimer (primary salvage platform, ~420 SCU combined grid)
Optional escort fighter or interdiction ship for the 5th player
Optional cargo hauler/snub to ferry filled crates to market faster
activities.reward: Money-positive industry. RMC sells roughly 6,000-7,700 aUEC/SCU depending on patch/location (verify live); a full grid is a strong multi-hour group payday split across the crew. Construction Materials and stripped components add more. Confidence: community for exact prices.
activities.start: Buy/rent or pledge-claim a Reclaimer (rentals at salvage-friendly hubs), spawn it, then hunt targets: scrape-panel derelicts in space, abandoned hulks, or take salvage contracts. Fly out as a crew, fracture and munch, then sell RMC at the best nearby market (e.g. Grim HEX or a TDD). See our Earnings and Field Guide tabs for live prices and target locations.

sources.label: Reclaimer — Star Citizen Wiki (ship spec, claw, fracture/disintegrate, 420 SCU, 5 crew)
Salvage — Star Citizen Wiki (RMC, structural salvage, filler stations)
Guide:Salvaging — Star Citizen Wiki
SCORG — Org Salvage Operations & Profit guide 2026 (crew roles, escort)
GGWTB — SC 4.8 Salvaging Guide (RMC profit, panel strategy, what changed)
UEX Corp — Recycled Material Composite (RMC) live sell prices

activities.name: Vulture / MOTH Salvage Convoy
activities.overview: Park a couple of compact salvagers wing-to-wing on the same wreck field and strip hulls together: everyone scrapes Recycled Material Composite (RMC), then pools the haul and sells as one big load. It's a relaxed, talkative industry grind that scales cleanly with crew — perfect for a small group that wants steady income while chatting, and it gets genuinely efficient once you add an Argo MOTH so multiple gunners blast the same hull from different angles instead of one beam doing all the work.
roles: Lead salvager / Vulture pilot (scrapes RMC, sets the wreck rotation)
Second Vulture or MOTH pilot (positions the medium salvager, runs the salvage arms)
MOTH turret gunner(s) (manned S2 salvage beams, parallel hull stripping)
Hauler / seller (consolidates RMC into one ship, runs the TDD sell-off)
Escort / overwatch (only for contested or higher-sec-risk wreck fields)
ships: Drake Vulture (one per solo salvager — the workhorse)
Argo MOTH (medium salvager, 3x S2 salvage turrets + 224 SCU, shines with 3-4 crew)
Optional escort fighter (e.g. a light/medium combat ship) for contested wrecks or PvP zones
Optional cargo hauler to consolidate and run RMC to market
activities.reward: ~100k-300k aUEC/hr for a solo Vulture; a coordinated 2-3 ship convoy stacks roughly that per active salvager, so a full session can net several hundred thousand to ~1M aUEC. RMC sells at roughly single-digit aUEC per unit but stacks fast; component pulls add 10k-50k each. (Rates are community estimates and shift with patches.)
activities.start: No mission giver needed — it's a free-form industry play. Each player buys/rents a Vulture (or one MOTH for the crew) from a ship terminal, fits salvage modules, and heads to a known wreck field or panel-rich derelict area. Scrape RMC (drop shields first on intact hulls), box it up, then sell at a city Trade & Development Division (Area18, New Babbage, Orison) — or GrimHex / salvage yards if you don't mind lower prices. Use the Earnings/Fleet tabs to plan ships and the route.

sources.label: Salvage - Star Citizen Wiki (hull scraping, RMC, selling at TDDs)
MOTH - Star Citizen Wiki (medium salvager specs, turrets, crew)
Argo MOTH Guide: Specs, Crew Roles & Salvage Ship Comparison - StarshipDealers
Star Citizen 4.8 Salvage Guide: Best RMC Farming & Ships - GGWTB
Hull Stripping & Structural Salvage with a Drake Vulture - Noob Citizen
Star Citizen Salvage Guide 2026: Tools & Profit - BoostRoom (profit/hr estimates)

activities.name: Multi-Crew Cargo Hauling Convoy (C2 / Hull C with crew)
activities.overview: One player normally grinds hauling contracts solo and burns most of their time wrestling freight elevators. Share that contract with the crew and it becomes a real cargo company: pilots dock and manage the ship, loaders work the freight elevators in parallel to fill the grid fast, and an escort flies overwatch on the route. Same payout per box, far less drag time, and the chatter on comms turns a chore into a co-op session.
roles: Pilot / freighter captain (flies the C2 or Hull C, docks, manages the cargo grid)
Loader (works freight elevators, moves SCU on and off so the grid fills fast)
Second loader / co-pilot (splits the other elevator, runs scans and power on a Hull C)
Escort pilot (light fighter on overwatch for the route and unload)
Logistics / quartermaster (stacks and tracks contracts on mobiGlas, calls the next stop)
ships: Crusader C2 Hercules (696 SCU, drive-on ramps, fast surface hauler)
MISC Hull C (4608 SCU telescopic-spindle bulk freighter for the big jobs)
Crusader A2/M2 or another C2 as a second hauler for convoy volume
A light fighter for escort (e.g. Gladius, Arrow, F7C Hornet)
MISC Hull A/B or a smaller hauler for newer crew who don't own a C2
activities.reward: Per-box payout is split evenly across the shared party. Routine Covalex/bulk-grade hauling realistically nets a few hundred thousand to low millions aUEC per session; community guides report up to roughly 7-11M aUEC/hour on optimized bulk routes, but only with senior reputation and heavily stacked contracts (community estimate, varies by route and patch).
activities.start: Open the Contracts app on your mobiGlas, take a Hauling contract (Small / Supply / Bulk grade) from a hauling employer such as Covalex; the party leader uses the "Share" button so rewards and reputation split evenly. Pick up cargo at the origin terminal via the freight elevators, load the grid, fly to the drop-off, and unload. Stack several contracts along one route for efficiency (Member rank can hold up to 16 active hauling missions). In-app, the Earnings/Trade tab covers the money loop.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Hauling (contract grades, freight, ships)
RSI Knowledge Base - Missions and Contracts (contract sharing)
Star Citizen Wiki - Contract (sharing rewards/reputation with party)
Star Citizen Wiki - Hull C (4608 SCU, telescopic spindle, crew 1-3)
Star Citizen Wiki - C2 Hercules Starlifter (696 SCU, ramps)
Tribality - SC 4.8 Covalex Cargo Hauling Guide (routes, reputation, aUEC tips)
ggwtb - SC 4.8.1 Money Guide (hauling aUEC/hour estimates)

activities.name: Risky Cargo / Smuggling Run Through Contested Space
activities.overview: Load a hold full of illicit cargo and run it through the lawless gap between Stanton and Pyro, where pirates camp the jump points and UEE security wants to scan you. One crew, one ship, real tension: somebody flies the line, somebody mans the guns, somebody watches the scanner and calls the route. It is the closest thing the 'verse has to a Han Solo run, and the payouts climb fast when nobody gets boarded.
roles: Pilot / nav - flies indirect quantum routes, dodges interdictions, picks the run-in to the sell point
Systems / scanner - watches contacts, manages power and shields, calls incoming and handles the handheld tractor for load/unload
Turret gunner(s) - mans the manned turrets to fend off pirates and any security response
Escort wing (optional) - a light fighter flying overwatch on the hauler through the contested jump
ships: MISC Starfarer Gemini (armored, 6 turrets, ~291 SCU - the brief's hero ship; wants 4+ crew)
RSI Constellation Taurus (tankier armed hauler that scales to 2-3 players)
Crusader C2 Hercules + a fighter escort (max cargo, needs a wingman for guns)
Drake Cutlass Black or Caterpillar (budget armed haulers for a 2-3 player crew)
activities.reward: Community reports put stacked Dead Saints illegal-cargo hauls around 300k-600k aUEC per mission, with larger multi-mission stacks going higher when the server cooperates (rough, varies by stack and patch). Straight drug running (e.g. SLAM Lyria→Wala) nets tens of thousands per small load. Treat all figures as rough ranges.
activities.start: Pick up illegal/unverified interstellar contracts from the Dead Saints faction (drug and contraband hauls into and out of Pyro and Stanton) via the contract manager / mobiGlas; you can stack the same haul across two players (one sharer, one holder). Free-trade smuggling instead: buy contraband at a lawless hub and sell at fences like Grim HEX (Yela), Samson & Son's (Wala), Brio's Breaker Yard (Daymar) or CRU-L4/L5. Plan the run in the Trade tab, kit the ship in Optimizer.

sources.label: Star Citizen Alpha 4.8 patch notes (RSI) - current live patch, refueling rebuild and Tactical Strike Groups
Star Citizen Alpha 4.8.0 Update (SC Wiki)
Starfarer Gemini (SC Wiki) - cargo, crew, turret loadout
Starfarer (SC Wiki) - crew of at least four (pilot plus gunners)
Smuggling (Star Citizen Wiki, Fandom) - career overview, shielded contraband transport
CrimeStat (SC Wiki) - scans, Comm-Array reporting, lawless vs monitored space
SC Crime System Guide (Space Vendor) - security scans, contraband confiscation, avoiding scans
Star Citizen drug running and selling cheat sheet (Citizen History) - sell points and loads (e.g. SLAM Lyria to Wala)
SC 4.8.1 aUEC Guide: Dead Saints Hauling (GGWTB) - stacked illegal hauls, payouts, 2-player setup
High-Paying Dead Saints Haul w/ Mobile Distribution Center 4.8.1 (StarZen)

activities.name: Refuelling Support Runs (Starfarer Crew)
activities.overview: You fly a MISC Starfarer out to stranded ships, deploy the fuel boom and pump hydrogen/quantum into their tanks for cash. It splits naturally into a crew job: one person flies and lines up the boom while a dedicated quartermaster works the pod-management console (picking pods, setting flow rate, watching for overflow). The big-ticket "Urgent Fleet Refuel" contract drops you on a beacon with a whole burned-out fleet to top up while hostile waves spawn, so an escort wingman turns a tense solo grind into a genuinely fun team op.
roles: Pilot - station-keeping next to the client and boom alignment
Quartermaster/boom operator - pod selection, fuel rate, avoiding overflow damage
Escort fighter - screen off pirate waves and fake-beacon ambushes
Spare turret gunner / second Starfarer pilot to clear multi-ship contracts faster
ships: MISC Starfarer (recommended)
MISC Starfarer Gemini
A second Starfarer for big fleet-refuel contracts
Light fighter escort (e.g. an Arrow/Gladius/Hornet)
activities.reward: Tiered support work: standard Refuel / Urgent Refuel Request jobs pay on the order of tens-of-thousands aUEC each and are mainly a reputation/roleplay loop. Occasional high-value fleet-refuel contracts exist, but the multi-million-per-hour figures circulating in the community are unverified and patch-dependent — treat refueling as modest, steady income, not a top earner.
activities.start: Need a MISC Starfarer or Starfarer Gemini plus reputation with the United Wayfarers Club. Grind low-tier refuel contracts (around Crusader/Hurston) up to Senior Contractor (~6,800 rep), then pick up "Urgent Fleet Refuel" in Pyro — community guides recommend basing out of Checkmate Station for fast restock/recovery. Contracts appear in the in-game Contract Manager (mobiGlas); in our app the Earnings tab.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Refueling (mechanics, fuel boom, solo vs crew operation)
Star Citizen Wiki - Starfarer (crew of 8, fuel boom, pod storage)
MMOPIXEL - SC 4.8 Refueling Missions Guide (contract tiers, rep, Checkmate, escort advice)
GGWTB - SC 4.8 aUEC Farming with Refueling Contracts (Urgent Fleet Refuel payout, multi-ship beacon, hostile waves)
Star Citizen Wiki API - Urgent Fleet Refuel mission (United Wayfarers Club)

activities.name: Armed Escort / Convoy Protection
activities.overview: One or two players fly a fat, slow earner — a cargo hauler or a Starfarer on a refuel contract — while the rest screen them in fighters. It turns a lonely, defenceless grind into a tense team op: the haulers focus on the money loop while gunners and interceptors swat NPC ambushers and shoo off player pirates. Great for crews because the value is real (the protected ship is doing the paying work) and every seat has a clear job, especially on high-tier Pyro runs where getting caught alone means losing the whole haul.
roles: Hauler/refueller pilot (drives the protected ship, runs the contract)
Escort lead/interceptor (screens ahead, breaks interdictions, calls targets)
Wing fighter (engages ambushers, covers the hauler's blind side)
Turret gunner or boarding repel (rides the big ship, fends off pirates trying to board)
QT scout/anchor (scouts the route, pre-clears the next jump point)
ships: Cargo hauler being protected: Constellation Taurus or Argo RAFT
Refuel variant: MISC Starfarer / Starfarer Gemini
Escort fighters: Aegis Gladius (balanced) or Anvil Arrow (fast interceptor)
Heavy escort: Anvil F7C-M Super Hornet or F7C Hornet Mk II for staying power
activities.reward: Highly variable, community-reported. Dedicated "Protect Ship" / Foxwell security contracts ~60k aUEC each at yellow tier, more at orange/red. The escort's real payday is the protected loop: combat-protection runs can average ~1M aUEC/hr, and a Starfarer on the Pyro Urgent Fleet Refuel contract (~1.8M aUEC, needs Senior Contractor rep) can clear 5M+ aUEC/hr with an escort clearing ambushers. Tag: community estimates, server-dependent.
activities.start: No single "escort" board entry drives this — the crew builds it. Pick the money loop first (cargo or refuel contract from the Contracts/mission manager, or Covalex cargo runs), then assign escorts. Stand-alone Protect Ship / security contracts appear in the contract manager once you have combat rep. In-app, plan it under Tactics (route + role split) and check Earnings/Trade for the cargo or refuel loop the convoy is protecting.

sources.label: Tribality — SC 4.8 Covalex Cargo Hauling Guide (piracy/escape, escort advice)
ggwtb — SC Patch 4.8 aUEC Farming with Refueling Contracts (Pyro 1.8M refuel + escort, 5M/hr)
RSI Community Hub — Protect & Escort Ship Missions (Foxwell ~60k payouts)
Starfarer — Star Citizen Wiki (refuel role, design, piracy vulnerability)
Gladius — Star Citizen Wiki (light fighter / escort role)
5GGM — Best Fighter Ships in Star Citizen 2026 (Gladius/Arrow/Super Hornet escort roles)
TEST Squadron — Space Combat Convoy for Dummies (convoy planning, leadership, role split)

activities.name: Medical & Rescue Response
activities.overview: One player flies a dedicated medical ship as a mobile hospital while the rest of the crew secures the crash or firefight, drags downed teammates back to the beds, and answers rescue beacons from strangers in distress. It turns "someone died" from a run-ending disaster into a quick recovery, so your group can push harder into dangerous content knowing a revive is always inbound. The support role is genuinely fun in a crew because the medic is the lifeline everyone depends on, and racing a bleed-out timer to a beacon makes for tense, heroic moments.
roles: Medic/pilot - flies the ship, runs the AutoDoc and beds, keeps the bird hot for dust-off
Combat escort - flies a fighter or holds the LZ so the medic isn't shot down on approach
Field medic/extractor - on foot with a Med Gun, stabilizes and carries downed players to the ship
Spotter/comms - reads the long-range radar and beacon board, calls navigation and threats
Reserve gunner/backup pilot - mans turrets or a second ship for hot extractions
ships: Drake Cutlass Red (entry medical ship, Tier 3 beds + AutoDoc, can stabilize but not fully revive)
RSI Apollo Triage or Apollo Medivac (dedicated, scalable to Tier 1 beds for full revive; Medivac is the combat-hardened CSAR variant)
An escort light fighter (e.g. Gladius / Arrow) for the combat role
A ground vehicle (e.g. ROC/Ursa-class) for reaching crash sites on foot
activities.reward: Rescue-beacon missions pay roughly 15,000 aUEC per delivery per official RSI support docs, with community reports citing up to ~50,000 aUEC; main value is keeping a crew alive and saving lost cargo/loadouts rather than raw income.
activities.start: No quest-giver needed: spawn a medical ship (Cutlass Red ~1.8M aUEC in-game, or rent an Apollo), then either embed with your own crew for combat/industry runs or watch the Service Beacon board for incapacitated players broadcasting a rescue beacon. Accept the beacon, fly to the marker, revive with a Med Gun + Hemozal, and deliver the patient to a medical bed or hospital for payout.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki - Medical (tiers, beds, revive loop)
Star Citizen Wiki - Cutlass Red (Tier 3 beds, AutoDoc, role)
Star Citizen Wiki - Apollo Medivac (CSAR, modular beds to Tier 1)
RSI Knowledge Base - Healing and Medical Treatment (beacon payout)
Star Citizen Wiki - Service beacon
StarCitizenHelp - Medical Gameplay Guide (2026)

activities.name: Combat Search-and-Rescue Under Fire
activities.overview: One crew member goes down hard inside a hot bunker or a contested wreck, and the rest of the team flies in to pull them out before the bleed-out timer runs dry. A medic ship sets down under fire while gunners and a boarding element suppress the NPC defenders long enough for the medic to hit the downed player with a revive beam and drag them to a medical bed. It is pure adrenaline role-split: someone is always shooting, someone is always healing, and the clock is always ticking. It turns an ordinary "man down" moment into the most memorable mission of the night.
roles: Medic ship pilot / dustoff (lands the bird, holds the LZ, flies the patient to a bed)
Combat medic (revives with Med Gun/ParaMed, drags and stabilizes the downed player)
FPS suppressor (clears and holds the room around the casualty)
Door gunner / turret (suppresses NPC defenders and any inbound ships)
Overwatch escort pilot (kills bunker turrets and screens for PvP interlopers)
ships: Drake Cutlass Red (accessible Tier 3 medbed dustoff ship)
RSI Apollo Medivac (armored combat-SAR ship, Tier 1/2 beds, missile racks)
RSI Apollo Triage (Tier 2/3 medbeds, lighter)
A light fighter or gunship as escort (e.g. F7C Hornet / Drake Cutlass Blue) for turret and PvP screening
Ground bike (e.g. Greycat ROC variants/Mule not needed; a hoverquad) to close distance to the bunker
activities.reward: Mostly for the thrill and teamwork, not the payout. If the casualty triggers an in-game rescue beacon, the responder is paid roughly 50,000 aUEC from the downed player's own account on hospital delivery (confirmed mechanic). Player-organized rescues during a bunker/wreck run pay nothing directly, but salvaging the original mission reward (often tens to a few hundred thousand aUEC for bunker clears) keeps the run profitable. Ranges; tag community.
activities.start: Self-organized: start any FPS bunker or wreck-clearing run (legal "Defend/Eliminate" bunker contracts on Hurston or microTech, or a salvage/PvP wreck), then improvise the rescue the moment a teammate goes down. For a structured version, the casualty triggers the rescue beacon from the incap screen so a medic gets a mission marker. Plan loadouts in the gear and shipbuy tabs; brief roles in tactics. App tab: tactics.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Medical (rescue beacon, ~50k aUEC payout, revive mechanics)
Star Citizen Wiki — Apollo Medivac (combat search-and-rescue role, armor, missile racks, bed tiers)
Star Citizen Wiki — Cutlass Red (Tier 3 medbed dustoff ship)
Star Citizen Wiki — ParaMed Medical Device (Med Gun revive/treat modes, Hemozal)
RSI — How To Ace Bunker Missions (FPS bunker NPC defenders, group coordination)
30k.fun — FPS Combat Guide for Ground Missions
GameRant — Star Citizen Medrunner / medical rescue guide
RSI — Star Citizen Alpha 4.8 patch notes (current LIVE patch context)

activities.name: Derelict & Abandoned Outpost Exploration
activities.overview: Fly out to dead ships, crashed wrecks and deserted settlements, then comb them on foot for loot crates holding armor sets, FPS weapons, medical supplies, rare multi-tools and the occasional ship component. The pull here is the eerie atmosphere and the shared hunt: the loot is heavily RNG-based, so one room can drop a sniper rifle and rare armor while the next has nothing but food crates, which makes every find a small group celebration. Splitting up to clear a site faster while someone watches the door turns a quiet scavenge into genuine teamwork.
roles: Pilot / dropship & extraction
Lookout / overwatch for NPCs and rival players
Looter / crate-clearer with tractor multi-tool
Hauler / inventory manager for the cargo run home
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (room for crew + cargo)
MISC Freelancer / Constellation (multi-seat hauler)
RSI Aurora / Drake Cutter (cheap solo entry per player)
Greycat ROC or ATLS for surface hauling (optional)
activities.reward: Variable, loot-driven rather than a fixed payout. A good run yields rare armor sets, FPS weapons (e.g. sniper/railgun-tier finds), the occasional Phantom-style multi-tool and medical/utility gear worth tens of thousands of aUEC if sold or kept; dry runs are common due to RNG and picked-clean popular sites. Any salvageable RMC sells around ~6,000 aUEC/SCU in 4.8 (community figure, down from prior patches). Confidence: community.
activities.start: No mission-giver needed: fly to known derelicts and abandoned/unmarked outposts (e.g. Pyro's Bloom has three outposts not shown on the starmap; Hurston and Stanton derelict outposts; scattered wreck sites). Find them via ship scanners over remote terrain, community location guides, or by pulling derelict-investigation / salvage contracts from a hub mission board. In the companion app this lives under the Field Guide tab for site lists and the Gear tab for FPS/tool loadout prep.

sources.label: 30k.fun - Exploring Derelict Ships and Stations (how-to, gear, hazards)
Massively OP - Alpha 4.8 'Tactical Strike' group PvE on an abandoned station
RSI - Star Citizen Alpha 4.8 patch notes
RSI Community Hub - Guide to Abandoned Colonial Outposts
GGWTB - Star Citizen 4.8 Salvaging Guide (RMC value ~6,040 aUEC/SCU)
MMOGah - Lazy Scavenger's Guide to Bloom (unmarked Pyro outposts, rare armor)

activities.name: Cave System Expedition (Hand + ROC Mining)
activities.overview: Drop your crew at a moon cave mouth and spelunk down together to harvest crystalline gemstones — Hadanite, Aphorite and Dolivine — with handheld multitools while a ROC cracks the bigger nodes topside. It's a relaxed, exploratory grind that turns into genuine teamwork: someone navigates the dark tunnels, someone fills backpacks, and someone keeps a watch since caves get cramped, disorienting and occasionally dangerous. Low pressure, good loot, and great voice-chat fun.
roles: ROC driver (cracks medium crystalline nodes near the entrance)
Hand miner / gem fracturer (multitool, deep cave deposits)
Hauler / pack mule (ferries full backpacks back to the ship)
Navigator + lookout (lights, mapping, watches for cave-dwellers and intruders)
ships: Greycat ROC (single-seat surface miner)
Greycat ROC-DS (two-seat, 3.4 SCU)
Drake Cutlass Black or RSI Nomad (carries the ROC + crew)
Greycat mining multitools with handheld attachment
Greycat PTV / ground bike for fast surface hops between cave mouths
activities.reward: Community-reported: a full hand-mining backpack run of mostly Hadanite is roughly 50,000–60,000 aUEC per ~30 min; a high-quality ROC-DS load (3.4 SCU) can reach the high six figures to ~1.8M aUEC at peak Hadanite prices (~437k–552k aUEC/SCU). Highly volatile — depends on gem quality, location and live market.
activities.start: Pick a moon listing gem resources (e.g. Daymar, Cellin, Aberdeen), load a ROC into a Cutlass/Nomad, and fly down. Find a cave entrance (community cave maps/guides help), land nearby, then descend on foot with multitools while the ROC works surface nodes. Sell at any Trading & Shipping Console (city or station). In-app: see the Earnings tab for current gem sell prices.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Mining
SC Mining Mom — Gemstone locations (caves)
Star Citizen Wiki — ROC-DS (dual-seat, 3.4 SCU)
GGWTB — 4.7.1 Hadanite & Dolivine mining money guide
MMOPixel — Best Gems Farm / Hand Mining Beginners Guide
UEX — Hadanite commodity prices
RSI Spectrum — New cave locations thread

activities.name: Racing Night at the Snake Pit (Clio) & Other Tracks
activities.overview: Grab light racers and turn a slice of the 'verse into a circuit. The Snake Pit is an abandoned open-pit mine on Clio (a moon of microTech) that the community and Wildstar Racing canonized into a rally course full of pylons, cranes and pipework to thread at speed. As a crew you can chase clean time-trial laps solo-but-together, then settle scores in head-to-head heats and grudge matches. It is pure skill-and-banter flying: no grind, low stakes, high "did you SEE that crash" energy — the ideal palette-cleanser between heavier ops.
roles: Pilot / racer (everyone flies)
Timekeeper & lobby host (sets heats, calls splits)
Spotter / commentator (calls lines, hazards, photo-finishes)
Pit / respawn buddy (helps reclaim or reset wrecks between heats)
ships: Origin 350r
Origin M50 Interceptor
Aopoa / Aegis Fury LX
Mirai Razor / Razor LX
Kruger P-52 Merlin
Consolidated Outland Mustang Gamma
Origin X1
activities.reward: For fun / bragging rights. No reliable aUEC payout from informal track nights; official Wildstar Racing time-trial missions grant reputation and small completion rewards rather than meaningful income.
activities.start: Two ways to run it. (1) In the Persistent Universe: fly to the Snake Pit on Clio (Stanton) and freestyle laps, or pick up Wildstar Racing time-trial missions (first one near Lorville on Hurston) via the mobiGlas Contracts manager — wear a helmet, the lap timer only shows on the helmet HUD. (2) For clean head-to-head heats with no server interference, host an Arena Commander "Classic Race" private/custom lobby (Ship or Vehicle, up to 8 players) and invite the party. Closest app tab: fieldguide (this is a location/activity guide entry, not an earnings or loadout calculator).

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — The Snake Pit (track on Clio, Wildstar Racing)
Star Citizen Wiki — Racing (time trials, Wildstar Racing reputation, helmet HUD timer)
Star Citizen Wiki — Wildstar Racing org & rally tracks
SCR (Star Citizen Racing) — Snake Pit track page
RSI Knowledge Base — Arena Commander (Classic Race, up to 8 players, private custom match)
RSI — Star Citizen Alpha 4.8 patch notes

activities.name: Planetary Road Trip / Overland Rover Convoy
activities.overview: Load a couple of ground vehicles into a hauler, pick a planet or moon, drop down somewhere remote and just drive together to a distant landmark. It is the closest thing Star Citizen has to a road trip: no clock, no mission timer, just terrain, scenery and good comms chatter. Small crews love it because everyone gets a seat and a job, and the convoy format means nobody is left flying solo while one person does the fun part.
roles: Hauler pilot (flies the C2/Cutlass down, picks the landing zone, runs orbital overwatch)
Lead driver / navigator (sets the route to the landmark, reads the map)
Second rover driver (keeps the convoy together, runs sweep)
Turret gunner / spotter (mans the Ursa turret, scouts terrain and wildlife)
Passenger / DJ / photographer (rides shotgun, handles music, screenshots, recovery if a rover flips)
ships: RSI Ursa Rover (6-wheel all-terrain, 4 jump seats - the convoy workhorse)
Tumbril Cyclone (light dual-seat buggy for a faster scout element)
Crusader C2 Hercules Starlifter (drive-through bay, ~696 SCU, carries multiple rovers to the surface)
Drake Cutlass Black (budget option - rear ramp fits one ground vehicle)
activities.reward: For fun - no in-game payout. It is a self-directed scenic/social activity, not a paid mission.
activities.start: No mission giver - this is an emergent, player-run activity. Spawn or rent ground vehicles (e.g. Ursa, Cyclone) and load them into a hauler with a vehicle-capable bay at any major station/hangar, fly to a planet or moon, land at a chosen drop point and drive overland to an agreed landmark, outpost or vista. Plan it like a meetup in the Field Guide; ground vehicles can be rented in-game if no one owns one.

sources.label: SC Wiki - Ground vehicles (must be carried by larger ships; gravlev for fast traversal)
SC Wiki - Ursa (all-terrain rover, 4 passenger jump seats, ground exploration)
SC Wiki - C2 Hercules Starlifter (drive-through bay, 696 SCU, vehicle transport)
SC Wiki - Cutlass Black (rear ramp fits a ground vehicle, ~46 SCU)

activities.name: Sightseeing / Scenic Tour Flights
activities.overview: Pile your friends into one big multi-crew ship and just cruise the system as a flying lounge: bank past Yela's asteroid rings, drop into Orison's cloud-city sunsets over the Crusader gas giant, skim ArcCorp's neon skyline at Area18, then watch a planetary sunrise from low atmosphere. One person flies while everyone else rides along, chats, and grabs screenshots. It's the lowest-stakes way to show new players how gorgeous the verse is without grinding or risking a single cargo box.
roles: Pilot / tour guide (plans the route, handles quantum jumps and atmospheric flight)
Co-pilot / navigator (sets quantum markers, calls out the next landmark, watches scanner)
Passengers / lookouts (ride in seats or roam the lounge, screenshot, vibe, optional turret watch if a PvP zone is on the itinerary)
ships: Origin 600i Touring (purpose-built luxury sightseeing yacht with strut-free wraparound canopy and a guest lounge)
Anvil Carrack (sub-capital explorer, huge windows and crew space for a bigger group)
RSI Constellation Andromeda/Phoenix (mid-size, comfortable, gets a crew anywhere)
Drake Cutlass Black (cheap, flexible 2-3 player budget option)
activities.reward: For fun / non-monetary — no payout. Value is the scenery, screenshots, and a relaxed social session; great onboarding for new players.
activities.start: No mission giver needed — this is self-organized. Spawn or claim a multi-crew ship at any major hangar (e.g. Everus Harbor, Baijini Point, or a planet-side spaceport), have everyone board the one ship, and plan a route on the Starmap. Safe Stanton highlights: Orison on Crusader (sunset/sunrise gas-giant city), Yela's asteroid rings near Grim HEX, Area18 on ArcCorp, and microTech's snowy aurora. The fan tool's Field Guide tab covers the landmark list; venture into Pyro only if the crew accepts PvP risk.

sources.label: Star Citizen Wiki — Sightseeing and Travel Guide
Star Citizen Wiki — 600i Touring (luxury sightseeing yacht, panoramic canopy, guest lounge)
Game Rant — Best Multi-Crew Ships in Star Citizen
GGWTB — Star Citizen Exploration Guide: scenic landmarks (Yela rings, Pyro 4, Benny Henge)
RSI Knowledge Base — Locations in Star Citizen

activities.name: Piracy: Interdiction and Boarding
activities.overview: Classic space piracy run as a coordinated crew: yank a loaded hauler out of quantum travel, pin it so it can't jump away, knock out its thrusters, then storm aboard on foot to grab the cargo and fence it on the black market. It rewards real teamwork because no single ship does it all alone, and every member has a distinct job that has to land at the right moment. Note it is fully illegal: interdiction, boarding and trafficking all stack CrimeStat, so expect to be shot on sight at lawful ports and to need a CrimeStat wipe afterwards.
roles: Interdictor/EWAR — runs the Mantis quantum snare or a QED quantum dampener to drop the target out of quantum and keep it pinned
Pilot/attacker — disables the target's thrusters and shields with precise fire (soft-death, not blow it up) and fends off responders
Boarding party (1-2) — EVA or dock onto the disabled hull, clear any crew/NPCs in CQC, and pop the cargo bay
Cargo runner/getaway — tractor-beams the crates into the loot ship and hauls the haul to a fence before security arrives
ships: RSI Mantis (the only ship with the quantum snare — your interdiction anchor)
Drake Cutlass Black (cheap, versatile pirate workhorse: fights, docks, and hauls a bit of loot)
Drake Caterpillar (big cargo hold to swallow the stolen boxes — the iconic pirate hauler)
A light fighter or gunship (e.g. Aegis Redeemer or Anvil Hornet) for escort and to win the disable fight
Handheld multi-tool with tractor-beam attachment, plus a tug like the Argo SRV for moving heavy crates
activities.reward: Highly variable and player-reported. A single loaded hauler can net tens of thousands up to several hundred thousand aUEC in fenced cargo, but raw stolen goods sell at a steep black-market discount and a botched run can pay nothing. Expect a CrimeStat fine on the order of ~7,500 aUEC for the interdiction itself, plus the cost and risk if a crew member dies.
activities.start: No formal mission required — it's emergent: scout busy quantum-travel lanes between trade hubs in Stanton/Pyro, set the snare, and pounce. For a structured version of the boarding loop, take the unlawful "Breach and Steal" contract (board a disabled/occupied vessel and extract a cargo crate). Plan loadouts in the optimizer/gear tabs, then fence loot at black-market terminals at outlaw outposts like Grim HEX. Wipe CrimeStat at pay terminals (CS1-2) or Security Post Kareah (higher).

activities.name: Pyro Contested Zone Territory Fights
activities.type: contested-zone PvPvE loot raid / territory fight
activities.overview: Pyro's Contested Zones are free-fire PvPvE arenas inside three lawless stations (Checkmate, Orbituary, Ruin) where the rarest loot in the game lives and rival crews collide over it. Your team pushes through fuse doors, keycard checkpoints and NPC defenders while watching every corner for other players who want the same prize. It is a teamwork pressure-cooker: holding a chokepoint, reviving a downed buddy mid-firefight and covering the extraction is exactly the kind of coordinated chaos a small crew thrives on, and the payoff is rare FPS weapons, Grade-A components and Executive-Hangar ships.
roles: Fireteam point/breacher (pushes doors, soaks first contact)
Support/medic (revives, carries spare ammo + med pens, runs fuses)
Loot runner (grabs comp boards/keycards, hauls to a safe stash)
Overwatch/anchor (holds the chokepoint, watches the back door for rival players)
Ship escort/extraction pilot (keeps a fighter on station to cover the dustoff and contest the airspace)
ships: Drake Cutlass Black (cheap drop-ship, easy to re-buy if lost)
Anvil C8R Pisces (in-zone medical/respawn point)
Aegis Avenger Stalker / Drake Corsair (multicrew transport)
Dedicated fighter for escort (Hornet, Gladius or similar) to cover the airspace and extraction
activities.reward: Community-reported as some of the best in the game: rare/upgraded FPS weapons (railguns, grenade launchers), Grade-A ship components, RMC/CMAS and illicit goods worth high aUEC, plus the 7 comp boards that unlock an Executive Hangar ship reward (e.g. F8C Lightning, F7A Mk II Hornet, Cutlass Black, Corsair, Syulen). Exact aUEC payouts are not published; treat numbers as variable. Counter-risk: death inside the zone forfeits equipped and carried gear.
activities.start: Travel to the Pyro system and head to one of the three contested-zone stations (Checkmate, Orbituary, or Ruin). Approach slow and low to avoid station turrets, then work inward through fuse doors and keycard checkpoints (Supervisor stations are where you grab the keycards). No mission-giver needed - the zone itself is the activity. In-app, see the Tactics tab for the room-by-room breach plan and the Field Guide for what loot to expect.

activities.name: Fleet Week / Free Fly Community Events
activities.type: seasonal community event / social expo tour
activities.overview: Twice a year CIG throws open the 'verse with a Free Fly: anyone can play with just a free account, and 100+ ships sit on the show floor to rent for zero aUEC. The flagship is Fleet Week — Invictus Launch Week / Drake DefenseCon in May — with the giant IAE expo repeating in Nov/Dec. It is the perfect low-pressure crew hang: wander the convention hall together, rent ships you would never buy, pile into a multicrew capital ship and actually fly it, and tour walk-through capitals like the Idris. No grind, no death stakes — just showing friends the scale of the verse.
roles: Host/guide: knows the expo floor, herds the crew to rental kiosks
Pilot: rents and flies the big multicrew ship everyone boards
Co-pilot/turret: mans guns and stations on the shared ship
Newbie/tourist: along for the ride, learning the ropes (free-account friends)
ships: Any rented expo ship (free at kiosks, 48h)
A multicrew ship to share, e.g. Constellation, Carrack or 890 Jump
Idris / capital ship walk-through tours (no flying needed)
Starter ship to spawn in (Aurora/Mustang) before grabbing rentals
activities.reward: For fun — free ship rentals (0 aUEC, ~48h each) and free game access during the event; no payout
activities.start: Make a free RSI account and download the launcher during an active Free Fly. Spawn in, travel to the Bevic Convention Center at Area18 (ArcCorp); rental kiosks line the entry walls — stand by a ship, hold F, choose Rent (cost 0). The roster rotates manufacturers every ~48h. Watch the in-app earnings/events feed for live dates. App tab: fieldguide.
